reviews.title: size

title: Logitech M305 Gold Tendrils Wireless Mouse
description: This wireless mouse features 1000 dpi optical technology for responsive cursor control and 2.4GHz wireless technology for a reliable connection.

reviews.title: Nice design
reviews.text: I use this with my netbook to control it across the room. It is smaller than most other mouse. It goes easily in my travel bag. Nice design. I got tired of plain black.

reviews.title: One of the best everyday mice I've used
reviews.text:
I have input devices for drawing on my tablet, and I have a gaming mouse, but the Logitech M305 is an excellent mouse for every day use, so it's the one I use the most.

The mouse itself has very basic features,
* scroll wheel (mild clicking feedback)with side-click options
* battery status light (green when fully charged, red when almost out)
* two buttons
* an off switch
* a nano USB transmitter than stores inside the mouse
* uses a single AA battery

It does come in jazzy designs, and I liked the yellow/gold one the best. The one thing I might have wanted to change on this mouse is the on/off switch. The best nano-USB mouse I've ever had would automatically turn off when you stored the transmitter inside. With this one, be sure to flip the switch to off as well before you put it in your bag, or it will be down there draining the battery as it moves around.

title: seenda Wireless Bluetooth Mouse - Rechargeable Wireless Mouse Multi-Purpose (BT 4.0+USB+Type C 3-Way Connection) PC Mice Compatible with iPad iPhone Mac OS Android Windows - Blue
features: TRIPLE CONNECTION MOUSE FOR MULTI-PURPOSE -- A versatile wireless mouse can connect up to 3 computers via Bluetooth 4.0, USB Receiver and Type C Receiver. No lags at all and could switch easily by a single “click” between the different inputs

RECHARGEABLE MOUSE WITH LONG BATTERY LIFE -- This Mouse has a built-in rechargeable battery which means you can use it without running the risk of not have a couple of batteries at hand. Comes with USB and Type C cables for charging

BLUETOOTH MOUSE WITH UNIERSAL COMPATIBILITY -- Universal compatible with Windows(Win 8,Win 10 or above), Mac OS(Mac OS X 10.10 or above), Android (Android 4.3 or above)devices. For iPad/iPhone, please turn on the "Assistive Touch" before Bluetooth connection: Setting-Accessibility-Assistive Touch

TRAVEL MOUSE WITH QUIET & RESPONSIVE CLICK -- Compact size of 4.5*2.5*1.3inch, the multi-connection option and rechargeable features make this mouse perfect for travel, you can enjoy a comfortable mouse instead of putting up with your laptop trackpads

18 MONTH MONEY BACK OR REPLACEMENTS -- A 18-Month risk-free warranty comes with this mouse, you will get a free replacement or money back for your order if any issues while using it. Just remember that we stands behind all of our products

reviews.title: Cheap and useless.
reviews.text: This mouse feels so cheap, it wasn’t even worth giving a review but I wanted to warn others to not get this mouse. It’s so flimsy and made so cheaply, I didn’t waste anymore time with it so I am returning.

reviews.title: Flawless Bluetooth connectivity with Mac
reviews.text: My Logitech M310 (been using them for 10-15 years) could not connect properly with my USB-C/Thunderbolt MacBookPro, using their USB dongle, thru a USB adapter. Scroll wheel would NOT work, and Logitech tech support said they would not update drivers. 'Don't use an adapter.' But they aren't aware that new Macs don't have old-school USB ports? Anyone else have this problem?! Anyway... this Seenda connects directly with Bluetooth, and connects instantly. If it sleeps (after a few hours?) it wakes and re-connects with one click, in less than a second. I charged the battery when I bought it a few weeks ago, and forgot that I might need to charge it again one day. Still going. Downsides: it is much smaller and lighter than I am used to (the M310 is easily twice as thick/tall) so I am getting used to that. Took awhile to find the right DPI/resolution/speed (and the button to change that is in a precarious, easy-to-hit-by-mistake position. Overall a great recommend.

reviews.title: squeaks when scrolling - not for small hands
reviews.text: Loud clicks, SQUEAKS when scrolling, made for larger hands. You would think since it's pink it wouldn't be designed for a mans hands but here we are. Ultimately, the obnoxious squeaking while scrolling is why I am returning it.

reviews.title: Works great but... The wheel squeaks!
reviews.text: This mouse works great but the wheel squeaks!

reviews.text: Hard to go wrong with a Logitech mouse - at least I never have!  The color looks exactly as pictured and matches my Dell laptop multi-color hardcover nicely.  Good handling and lightweight, will see how long the battery lasts before I have to change it.

reviews.title: Great little mouse

reviews.title: Too cute!
reviews.text: Turned out the same color as I saw on my screen when I ordered it!!! So excited it matched the mouse pad I ordered too! Love it, works perfectly!

reviews.title: Works well, when it connects.  Update:  It loses connection WAY too much
reviews.text:
I like the mouse. Excellent value for money, except for one very annoying flaw. It is constantly disconnecting the bluetooth connection from my new Macbook Pro. It's pretty annoying.

After what feels like only a few minutes of idle time, the mouse bluetoothe disconnects. Then when I move it nothing happens for a couple seconds. The finally, it connects and my laptop tells me the mouse is back, and it works perfectly... until the next time it is idle.

I have use other low cost brands as well as my Apple Magic Mouse (which I don't use as I prefer a track wheel). I have not had any issues with the Bluetooth on those, only this one...

If it were not for the disconnect issue I would rate the mouse a 5 star. Otherwise, I give it a 3. I am still using it for now, but will find a better one...

UPDATE: Okay, I find that I am CONSTANTLY drustrated that this mouse loses connection with my laptop. PITA. Not worth using at any price.

reviews.title: Junk
reviews.text: Even after swapping out the battery, and making sure it’s in pairing mode, it refuses to pair with any device I’ve tried and doesn’t show up in my Bluetooth menu as a visible device. It very briefly showed up on my Macbook as a keyboard, but never as a mouse, and now it won’t even do that. Save your money and get a mouse that will work.

reviews.title: Excellent Keyboard!
reviews.text: This was a Christmas gift for my daughter and she absolutely loves everything about this keyboard. Thank you

reviews.title: Keyboard is very loud when typing!
reviews.text: The mouse is pretty standard, nothing great, but nothing bad. I brought my cute little set to work in my laptop bag so I figured it was secure. When I took the keyboard out 1 letter had popped off. No biggie, it snapped back on easily. The keyboard buttons are bit higher than I'm used to so I was typing slower and it was a bit uncomfortable after a while. I planned on switching back to my regular keyboard shortly but what escalated that switch was how loud the keyboard was while I was typing. My office is usually rather quiet plus I'm in a cubicle and a coworker asked what are you doing over there!?! He said it sounded like I was hammering the letters with a small hammer. I knew it was loud but after realizing it was bothering my coworkers I immediately put it in my bag to bring home. Haven't used it since!

reviews.title: Nice keyboard and mouse set
reviews.text: This is a great mouse and keyboard set, feels like typing on an old typewriter but more comfortable. I like the combined usb. Looks very nice, comfortable to use,

reviews.title: Amusing
reviews.text: I really like this set. They both match perfectly. The keyboard is comfortable and all the buttons work perfectly. I like the round buttons they are pretty cool. Connects easy enough. Just wish it was rechargeable.

reviews.title: Plug and Play Wireless Keyboard; Not sure about the mouse
reviews.text: I run Windows 10 on my laptop, and this wireless keyboard was up and running in few minutes after unpackaging.  You will need to provide your own batteries (2 AA – one for the mouse and one for the keyboard).  The keys are not tactile like other “typewriter” keyboard I have, but the keys have enough tension in the loaded spring to give a slight “satisfactory” feel to the keystrokes.  The wireless connection between the receiver and the keyboard/mouse was responsive and experienced no noticeable delay.  I am not 100% sold on the “milk tea” colored keyboard, but it slowly grew on me after a few days of use, although my 8-year young daughter thought it was like a puke color… an interesting observation.  The mouse was not for me.  It was too flat and too small for my hand, and after a few hours of cumbersome use, I switched back to my existing mouse pad.  For less than $49 USD, I think this keyboard brings a fun look to the otherwise a boring office environment.

title: Microsoft Wireless Mouse 5000
description:
Product Description
World’s Most Advanced Tracking — Go More Places Than Optical or Laser. The Microsoft Wireless Mouse 5000, with revolutionary BlueTrack Technology, works more places than ever before, including on granite countertops, carpets — even on a wood table or a rough, unfinished park bench.
From the Manufacturer
Microsoft Wireless Mouse 5000
Comfortable shape
World's Most Advanced Tracking - Go More Places Than Optical or Laser
The Microsoft Wireless Mouse 5000, with revolutionary BlueTrack Technology, works more places than ever before, including on granite countertops, carpets - even on a wood table or a rough, unfinished park bench.
BlueTrack Technology
Comfort for Either Hand
Work anywhere with the world's most advanced tracking.
Comfort and durability
Modern design and premium finish provides comfort for either hand. Symmetrical shape and unique rubber grips give you control and cushioning.
Snap in and Go
Simply snap in the transceiver for convenient on-the-go storage.
Reliable Wireless
Microsoft 2.4 ghz wireless delivers a reliable connection with up to a 30-foot range.
8-Month Battery Life
Use your mouse without power interruption for up to 8 months. The snap-in transceiver automatically turns the mouse on and off, and the led battery status indicator lets you know when replacement is needed.
What is BlueTrack?
BlueTrack Technology works on more surfaces than both optical and laser mice. It uses high-angle imaging optics to generate an accurate picture of the surface.
System Requirements

Windows 7, Windows Vista, Windows XP (excluding Windows XP 64-bit)

reviews.title: Cute item but it doesn't stay connected.
reviews.text:
Super cute item! It took me a while to get use to the small click space and it tracks well but the main issues is the USB isn't reliable. Anytime I put the laptop to sleep or I don't use the mouse for a while I have to unplug the USB and turn the mouse on/off. It is really inconvenient but my return wind has closed to exchange it.

Looks are a 10 and functionality is a 2 because it does not work as intended.

reviews.title: low quality
reviews.text: I received a replacement for the first one that would shut off and on constantly. The 2nd one worked for about a week and then stopped. When trying to remove the USB connector from my laptop it cam apart. Part of it got stuck in the USB port. It took over 5 minutes to pull the rest of it out with tweezers! I returned it for a refund. This is a low quality piece of junk. Do not buy.

reviews.title: Cheap- don’t buy it!
reviews.text: This mouse works some of the time... very cute but unreliable. For the return process, I had to pack and ship on my own. It cost half the price of the mouse to return.

reviews.title: Cutest thing ever
reviews.text: So adorable! It moves super smoothly and I love the color. It is wider than most mice I'd say, but not by much. It still feels natural and easy to use. The buttons are easy to use as well. My only concern is that when I have to wake my computer up from sleep mood, I really have to shake it. But that may be my sensitivity settings, but they were just fine with my previous mouse. Either way, this is super cute and I love it!

reviews.title: DESTROYS PORTS! DO NOT BUY!
reviews.text: The first one died after 2 hours. It damaged the port on one side of my laptop. The replacement did the same thing to the other side. I now have no functioning ports - can’t use another mouse or flash drives. This company owes me a new laptop!!!!

title: VELOCIFIRE V9 RGB Gaming Mouse, 9800 Optical Sensor 4 Adjustable DPI with 16.8 Million Color Backlit 8 Programmable Buttons Tunable Weights - Black
features: MUST HAVE - With advanced ergonomics that comfortably support your entire hand. The 8 programmable buttons can be customized with ease to fine tune your play.

ACCURATE RESPONSIVE - Control like you are in the game. Get maximum tracking accuracy from our most responsive optical sensor, minimizes mouse acceleration and increases reliable targeting. Switch DPI modes on the fly - choose from four settings from 100 to 8200 DPI.

BALANCE AND WEIGHT AT YOUR CONTROL - It’s your mouse the way you like it. Customize to your advantage. Three 4.3g weights come with your V9 Mice. Use some or all of them in the positions where you like more weight, or less. This mouse can be tailored to your preferences.
Programmable RGB Lighting - Adjustable RGB lighting lets you select from approximately 16.8 million colors. Use Velocifire Gaming Software to choose lighting color and brightness to match your style.

DPI SHIFT IN-GAME - Be ready in an instant. Be an unstoppable force with V9 Mice in either low or high DPI. Shift from headshot-precise 100 DPI targeting to lightning-fast 8200 DPI maneuvers by shifting through up to four DPI settings per profile.

reviews.title: faulty unit
reviews.text: The right click does not work, it seems to be a design flaw or its just so cheap.

reviews.title: I found the buttons too easy to click
reviews.text: Meh...I found the buttons too easy to click, sometimes just the weight of my hand would make the mouse click. Also, the mouse cursor would wander when the mouse itself wasn't moving. It has some features that surprised me like being able to take weights in and out of the mouse for customized weight, but the easy clicks and the wandering cursor were deal breakers. Overall it feels a bit cheap. I got a logitech 510 instead and really like that mouse. Just a casual gamer here, wasn't looking for anything fancy.

reviews.title: Great Mouse!
reviews.text:
I really like this mouse and for the money, it is by far the best value in a gaming mouse I have purchased to date. I have spent over $100 for other gaming mice just to have them wear out after a year of use. I paid about $20 for this mouse and I am not disappointed in the quality at all. It uses the same switches as more expensive mice, feels great in my hand and is just as responsive as those more expensive models I purchased in the past. You may not get all the RGB lighting effects or programming features, but for the cost it cannot be beat.

The only thing that is a slight disappointment is the scroll wheel. One click is too strong and there are no settings in the software to adjust it. For the price I paid, it is something I can easily overlook and still give this mouse a 5 star review.

reviews.title: Defective Mouse
reviews.text:
This mouse would rate five stars if it didn't go bad after the return date.

It feels nice in the hand, it does all the mouse things it is supposed to, and it slides around smoothly. That is, when it decides to connect to my computer. I have a macbook pro with a bootcamp partition and primarily plug in through a USB hub. At first, I thought the hub had issues, but my keyboard is also connected to the hub and it has zero problems connecting with either OS. The mouse, on the other hand, likes to go on strike, even when plugged directly into the laptop's USB port. I have yet to determine the conditions cause the mouse to stop working, but I have yet to find a pattern. The mouse is just as likely to stop working on El Capitan as it is on Windows 10.

I tried to contact Velocifire since my mouse started being lazy after the return window, but I never heard back. I will not be buying from this company again.

reviews.title: Good find!
reviews.text: I got this to keep in my work bag as a backup/when I work from home. I will say the first impression wasn't stellar. The top plastic has a cheap look/feel to it. But it also makes up in some nice touches. For example, the USB fob. I was apprehensive about the keep slot for it because there wasn't something that made it feel or look actually secured. But turns out, there's magnetic component so it gets sucked in rather than loosely placed. Battery power seems as advertised. I haven't charged mine in about a month and it's still going. Ergonomics is about what you'd expect based on the photos.

reviews.title: No more Batteries
reviews.text: Love my new mouse! Old one I would turn off and on all the time to save battery. This one I keep wanting to then remember it goes to sleep and no more batteries. First charge went fast, haven't had to charge it since. Love the self storing dongle. Should have purchased this long time ago.

reviews.title: Nice small keyboard

I just could not get use to the placement of the number keys, and arrow keys so was making too many errors so had to return it. Had it not been for that I would have given it 5 stars.

reviews.title: Among the smallest with full size keys/keyboard
reviews.text: This product has a small footprint and so takes up less desktop space.  But I found the mouse equally small to the point that I returned the product -- and I don't even have large hands.  So consider this before you buy.  Otherwise it's a good product.

reviews.title: Amazing addition to the Logitech G Pro Wireless!
reviews.text: Great quality, precision cut pieces that fit EXACTLY in the shape of the mouse. Whoever designed the CAD for this did a great job on the measurements. Using the grip itself, also crazy how something so small makes a big difference. No more worrying about it slipping off my right thumb which I mentally couldn't handle. It's shame that Logitech didn't design the grip but this will do nicely until they do.

reviews.title: They're ok.
reviews.text:
I'm only using the side grips. (No issue with the texture of the buttons, so imo, its best to just not use them and keep the mouse as light as possible)

Its definitely an improvement over the stock plastic feel, which is too smooth for me. These add some grip, and even a bit of cushion.

Guess I was expecting a more grippy feeling. Maybe a more pronounced texture. There is definitely room for improvement for those of us with sweaty gaming hands.

I also wish the edges were tapered off at its edges rather than a hard cut, but that's a minor nitpick and not too important.

I will say with these grips, I am having trouble deciding between which mouse to use primarily... G Pro Wireless or Viper Ultimate. This is definitely something that was missing from the G Pro Wireless's design for me.

reviews.title: Love it!

reviews.title: Very good price-point for Laptop portability and Features
reviews.text:
Rating this PINKCAT wireless mouse is difficult in terms of "stars". The price-point and your intended use are big factors.

I'll get one thing out of the way to save you some time - if you're a gamer, I would say that this is not going to have the stable accuracy you need. In fact, I think it will frustrate gamers, so if you're a gamer then you need to move on/up to something more expensive. Just my opinion.

My usage is for work. Lots and lots of work - like 12-15 hours a day of non-stop "click click click" work, so I'm basing my review on that and in comparison, to my main "workhorse" mouse.

My main mouse is a Logitech Anywhere MX, which is an incredible mouse but is also about $50 more than this PINKCAT mouse. I wanted to find something similar in both size and features as my more expensive mouse but with silent click and more as a backup, and for basic uses this fits the bill pretty well.

As you can see in the photos, this mouse is only slightly larger than a Logitech Anywhere MX, so it's still plenty small enough for laptop / portable / small space use. Now that you know what I'm comparing it to, I'll get right to the pros & cons.

PROS:

- Very light-weight, significantly lighter than my MX, yet still similar in size & shape.

- The "silent" buttons are truly that! Something I wish was a feature with my noisy MX buttons.

- Only requires one AA battery (my MX requires two) and excellent battery life.

- Scroll-wheel is smooth, quiet, and comfortable.

- The dull mat style surface is very comfy and doesn't get sticky like the shiny slick surface of my MX.

- The "back" and "forward" thumb-buttons work as expected for quick switching back & forth on web pages and tabs / locations in file explorer folders.

CONS:

- Even with the "3 Grade Adjustable DPI" function, I wouldn't recommend this mouse for gaming or fine digital artwork in apps like Photoshop.

- The signal and receiver seem to be a little easily susceptible to signal issues and occasional jitter, but that could be due to the number of various devices I have in the room. When I first tested it out there seemed to be a lot of jitter and the cursor jumped around a bit, but after switching it to a different system than the one I used my MX on, and adjusting the mouse settings a bit in Windows, it has been pretty smooth.

- No side-to-side push with the scroll wheel, unlike my MX. (But again, we're talking $8 compared to $58).

- Battery compartment door is flimsy. It's OK, but handle with care when popping it open.

Overall, this mouse is fine for basic use, and for its price-point it's excellent.

This was really hard to rate in terms of "stars" , especially coming from the perspective of someone who owns a more finely tuned & designed expensive mouse. So... for the price and for basic use I'd give it 5 stars, but for smooth accuracy it's not going to measure-up to something like a high-end Logitech and would be more like 3 stars in that aspect. But for 8 bucks It's a great mouse - gets the job done for standard computer uses, and it's definitely light, silent buttons! So over all I have to give it 4 stars.

title: Neises Ergonomic Mouse, Bluetooth Mouse for ipad,Bluetooth Mouse for MacBook Air/Mac/MacBook Pro/Mini/ipad Pro/iMac/Laptop,Rechargeable Wireless Mouse for MacBook Air/MacBook pro Chromebook Laptop PC
description:
Tip:Scientific ergonomic design maintains healthy wrist and arm positions to achieve smoother movement and reduce overall stress.
How to use it:
A. 2.4Ghz Mode:
Switch to "ON/OFF" button to right.And Plug the receiver into the USB port or use a USB C adapter to plug into the type c port of your computer, Then you can use the mouse in 2.4GHz Mode.
B. Bluetooth Mode:
Switch on Bluetooth on your device and power on the mouse. Press the DPI Button or Press Left,Roller and Right 3 Buttons together for 5 seconds.When the mouse name coming up on the device.Click it and it will be connected.
Note:
System Requirement: Windows 8/10, Android 4.2 and above,Mac & IOS 10.10(Compatiable with 4.2BLE、4.1BLE、4.0BLE or earlier)
Package Included:
1* Dual Mode Mouse
1* Charging Cable
1*USB Receiver (under earth the mouse)
1*Type C Adapter
Note:
1. It's out of power if the mouse cursor moves slowly or stop moving. Please recharge it. Note: Please do not charge the mouse with mobile phone charger.
2. Please charge the mouse for 2 hours before the first time to use it. The blue light beside the scroll wheel will be on while charging, and then goes off when the mouse is fully charged.
3. Not all computers support two modes. For example, Win 7 system does not support Bluetooth mode.
4. There is memory function on Bluetooth Type Mouse.When the mouse has connected another device,and now you would like connect the device not connected with the mouse last time, it should re-connect with the device.
5. If choosing Bluetooth connecting way, don’t insert USB receiver to the device you would to connect.Otherwise,it will choose 2.4G connecting way automatically.

reviews.title: Good but wanted more features
reviews.text:
I bought this in a time crunch. My wired mouse was getting hard to use because the scroll wheel had shifted out of place. I had several wireless mice around, but none of them still had their wireless receiver. And they were before the days of Bluetooth. So, I had was in the market for a new one.

This one is nice. It fits the hand well and all. But, I realized that it did not have the features that I was used to. First, it is not a quiet mouse. I did not notice at first, but when it penetrated my fog of concentration, I could not unhear it. Click. Click. Click! And, this was not great for conference calls either.

The other issue was that it tracked slower than I would like. I was still using my wired mouse at this point, so I did not want to change mouse settings via software. I really missed having a dpi button on my mouse. This is important to me as I use a laptop on an extended display to a 2nd monitor. So, I need to be able to zip from one end of the screen the other - quick enough to unmute and answer a question during a conference call.

Have to click to wake up the mouse kept catching my attention - which means it was on it's way to annoying me... I have worked with computers for so long, it's like an extension of me. It needs to be intuitive to use and not interrupt my flow.

And, at this point, I figured out that I would need to use two different computers at once. And, while I could pair this wireless mouse with a wired one, the USB ports required were a challenge. My newer computer has no USB C ports at all. So, this limited my set-up options. Secondly, I now had 2 mice crowding limited desk space.

So, I learned that there was such a thing as multi-device mice. And, I found one that can switch between two computers and a iPad - with a simple LED indicator so that you know which device you are controlling. I bought the also designed to be silent and to switch between different dpi. All these features and it was only $3 more.

This is what I bought to replace this one - Arteck Multi-Device Wireless Bluetooth Mouse with Nano USB Receiver Ergonomic Right Hand Silent Clicking for Computer Desktop PC Laptop Mac iPad and Windows 10/8 iPad OS Build in Rechargeable Battery

So, to sum it up, I would say that I need the speed and responsiveness of a gamer's mouse with the silent clicks and 3 device connects like an office worker. So, this mouse is ok. But, if you are an advance or intense user, you may find that it does meet your needs.

reviews.title: I'll never go back to a flat mouse
reviews.text: I was suffering from tennis elbow for months, badly, and nothing I did was making any difference.  I have a job typing about 10 hours a day, eight days a week:/ and did some research about posture and what could cause this.  Already had an ergo keyboard, so I bought this ergo mouse.  WOW.  Unbelievable how almost immediately the tennis elbow cleared up.  This was super easy to get use to.  I have small hands, and the size is perfect.  I will never use a flat mouse again.  So very glad I stumbled upon this.  Best money I've ever spent.  Oh, and I absolutely love the fact that I do not have to replace batteries every week!

I wanted the blue one forever, and years ago I got one in pink instead of blue per their mistake. I just kept it. I used it and yes it still works, I really wanted the blue one so I took my time to look for another one just like it and yes it is hard to find this blue color. Beautiful. The fit is very smooth and soft and perfect, they know what hands like to rest in a cupped shape and this is comfortable to work with. The glide is excellent and can be used for everyday, office, or gaming. Take a look around at other styles but you will not find one that fits like this ergonomically for the price which you cannot beat. The best part is that it is rechargeable and yes it comes with the cord and also you can use it while it is charging. Keep your wrist happy and get one.

reviews.text: Not working for MacBook properly, cursor struck frequently

title: Wireless Mouse, Led Mouse Portable Mobile Optical Cordless Mouse with USB and Type C Receiver, 3 Adjustable DPI Wireless Computer Mouse for Mac Book, Laptop, Desktop,Windows, PC - Black
features: 【Rechargeable Wireless Mouse】 Built-in durable rechargeable battery, it can be easily charged using the included USB cable without replacing the battery.

【Power Saving Mouse & 7 Colorful Light】 With energy saving function, cordless mouse will enter sleep mode after 5 minutes of inactivity. Just click and the mouse will enter working mode again; 7 Colorful Breathing Light Effect, can not set in one specific color, you can choose to turn it on or off.

【Silent Cordless Mouse】 Silent click Mouse with anti-fingerprint finish offers, Silent button reduces 90% noises, which makes it an ideal wireless mouse for office, conference room, help you concentrate on your work.

【3 DPI Switch Computer Mice】 Is more accurate than traditional USB mouse, working distance up to 10 meters, no need to install software can be used as a gaming mouse. 3 DPI switches (1000, 1200, 1600) freely change the mouse speed for all kinds of games.
【Universa Compatibility】with USB and type C receiver wireless mouse works with computer, laptop, desktop with USB, type-c port, plug and play.

reviews.title: The colors on ad appear to be  lit and like spider web. NOT!

reviews.title: Nice low profile...
reviews.text: The RGB cycles very nicely, battery life is great for an 8 hour 5 day work week, my hand feels relaxed when in use. Sleep mode is approx 10 minutes like most other devices...well worth the $.

reviews.title: Girlfriend loves it
reviews.text: Good value. My girlfriend loves anything rainbow so this was a win. She says she likes the slim factor because it fits in her case well

reviews.title: EXACTLY AS DESCRIBED!!
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:4b00888f14173e06e2b0a843f3cd822f]] I love this mouse! I love that it changes colors and just looks so cool! I love the spider design! It does stay charged for a long time!! I game for hours sometimes lol

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.text: Cool graphics as well as quick response. Nice wireless mouse with flawless connection.

title: Logitech Wireless Mouse M325 with Designed-For-Web Scrolling - Blue
description: The Logitech Wireless Mouse M325-a better mix of precision and comfort. With its designed-for-Web scrolling, now everything you love to do online is easier. Designed to improve your online experience, the wheel also helps perfect scrolling when you're using other applications, such as Microsoft Office or Adobe PDF. System Requirements: Windows 10 Windows 8 Windows 7 Windows Vista|Mac OS X 10.5 or later|Chrome OS|Linux kernel 2.6+|USB port
features: Micro precise scrolling: New designed for web scrolling makes searches, shopping and browsing easier
Feel good design: Contoured shape and textured rubber grips keep your hand comfortable even after long hours
Long battery life: Upto 18 months of battery life virtually eliminates the need to change batteries; Battery life may vary based on user and computing conditions
For windows, mac and linux: reliable wireless connection with tiny logitech unifying nano receiver that can stay in your laptop

reviews.title: Liked the mouse
reviews.text: I like the fun color and the ease of movement. Easy to install.  I would recommend this to other buyers.

reviews.title: great
reviews.text: best  mouse  I  have  ever  owned  and one of the  cheaper  ones  too!  It  is  small  and  easy  to move!

reviews.title: Great Little Mouse
reviews.text: This mouse is a nice compact size but doesn't feel too small in my hand.  I have medium/large hands for a guy.  Great for travel and daily use at the office.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Love this mouse.  I like how you can let the wheel fly to get to the top or the bottom.

reviews.title: No more cords
reviews.text: Cut down on cord clutter & use anywhere.  Won't go back to corded ones
